pattern: Every element is the maximum (or minimum) of a contiguous window exactly between its nearest larger neighbors (or smaller neighbors), so a monotonic stack turns 'which windows is this element responsible for' into two boundary indices, and capping the window length at k only trims how far the responsibility reaches.
hints: For each position, find the nearest position to its left with a strictly larger value and the nearest position to its right with a value at least as large -- that range is exactly the set of left endpoints and right endpoints for which this element is the maximum of the subarray. Mirror this with smaller values to get the minimum's range.

Without a length cap, the count of subarrays for which an element is responsible is simply the product of the left-range size and the right-range size -- but with the at-most-k cap, walk the possible left endpoints and clamp each one's reachable right endpoint at left + k - 1.

Sum each element's value times (how many times it is a maximum, plus how many times it is a minimum) across the whole array; do the max pass and the min pass independently and add the two totals together.

edge_inputs.description: single element, trivially its own max and min
k equal to 1, every subarray is a single element counted twice
k equal to the array length, every subarray up to the full length is included
repeated values must not be double counted across overlapping windows
a single very negative value should still register as a minimum
all values at the maximum bound

stress.description: random arrays across the full value range with a random window cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 4000)))
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000000, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minMaxSubarraySum(self, nums, k):
        n = len(nums)

        def bounds(strict_left_pop, strict_right_pop):
            left = [-1] * n
            stack = []
            for i in range(n):
                while stack and (nums[stack[-1]] <= nums[i] if strict_left_pop else nums[stack[-1]] >= nums[i]):
                    stack.pop()
                left[i] = stack[-1] if stack else -1
                stack.append(i)
            right = [n] * n
            stack = []
            for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
                while stack and (nums[stack[-1]] < nums[i] if strict_right_pop else nums[stack[-1]] > nums[i]):
                    stack.pop()
                right[i] = stack[-1] if stack else n
                stack.append(i)
            return left, right

        L_max, R_max = bounds(True, True)
        L_min, R_min = bounds(False, False)

        def contrib(i, L, R):
            low_l = max(L + 1, i - k + 1)
            total = 0
            part1_hi = min(i, R - k)
            if part1_hi >= low_l:
                cnt1 = part1_hi - low_l + 1
                total += cnt1 * (k - i) + (low_l + part1_hi) * cnt1 // 2
            part2_lo = max(low_l, R - k + 1)
            if part2_lo <= i:
                cnt2 = i - part2_lo + 1
                total += cnt2 * (R - i)
            return total

        ans = 0
        for i in range(n):
            ans += nums[i] * (contrib(i, L_max[i], R_max[i]) + contrib(i, L_min[i], R_min[i]))
        return ans
